pain_point_label: High Learner Heterogeneity & Undiagnosed Learning Gaps

matches.match_rationale: Student records — attendance, prior grades, assessment scores, demographic data — are structured tabular inputs that can be classified to flag at-risk learners or assign readiness tier labels before diagnostic testing exists. This directly addresses the 'no early diagnostic data' problem by surfacing latent signals already in the SIS/LMS. For a 500+ staff operator, this is the foundational layer: run classification on existing structured data to generate an early-warning tier (on-track / at-risk / urgent) per student without requiring new data collection.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A student risk-stratification model trained on SIS/LMS records that assigns each student a readiness tier at the start of each term, giving teachers a starting point for differentiation before formal diagnostics are completed.

matches.match_rationale: Predicting which individual students are likely to fall further behind — or fail to meet grade-level benchmarks — is a propensity modeling task: features about a specific entity (student) mapped to a probability of a future behavioral outcome (academic regression, non-completion). This directly targets the 'no systematic way to identify at-risk learners early' gap. Unlike classification which assigns a current state, propensity models forecast trajectory, giving educators lead time to intervene.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A per-student propensity score updated weekly that estimates the likelihood of falling more than one grade level behind by end of term, surfaced in a teacher dashboard with the top contributing signals.

matches.match_rationale: Once learner tiers are identified, teachers face the scale problem: they cannot manually write differentiated lesson materials, instructions, or scaffolding for 3-4 readiness levels in every class. Text generation directly addresses the 'teachers unable to differentiate at scale' mechanism by producing multiple versions of explanations, instructions, worked examples, and scaffolding prompts from a single lesson plan. This is technically trivial and culturally low-risk — teachers review and approve, AI drafts.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A teacher-facing tool that takes a lesson objective and generates differentiated instruction materials at 2-3 readiness levels (foundational, grade-level, extension), reducing teacher prep time for mixed-ability classrooms.

matches.match_rationale: Students who are 2+ grades behind cannot wait for teacher attention in a 30-person classroom. A conversational tutoring agent can provide targeted, adaptive explanation and practice at a student's actual level in real time — addressing both the heterogeneity and the 'struggling students fall further behind' mechanisms simultaneously. Multi-turn dialogue allows the agent to detect misconceptions and adjust scaffolding dynamically, which single-turn generation cannot do.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A student-facing AI tutor deployed on school devices that delivers adaptive practice sessions at each student's diagnosed level, with session summaries sent to the teacher flagging persistent misconceptions.

matches.match_rationale: Grouping students for differentiated instruction — matching students to teacher attention, peer groups, intervention slots, and resource availability — is a constraint satisfaction problem: multiple hard constraints (class size, teacher availability, room capacity, scheduling) and soft constraints (learning tier balance, social dynamics) must be satisfied simultaneously. This addresses 'teachers unable to differentiate at scale' by optimally configuring groupings the teacher would otherwise do manually and suboptimally.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A term-start grouping tool that, given student readiness tiers and scheduling constraints, generates optimal small-group configurations for differentiated instruction across a campus's classrooms.
